name: Frozen Orange Truffle Souffles
recipeIngredient: Orange liqueur (e.g., Grand Marnier or Orange Curacao), 3 tbsp
recipeInstructions:
Note: Grand Marnier or Orange Curacao are suggested orange liqueurs.
1. Wrap parchment paper or aluminum foil collars around six 2/3-cup souffle dishes and fasten with tape or string.
2. In a small bowl, sprinkle gelatin over orange juice and set aside until softened. Place the bowl in a pan of hot water and stir occasionally until the gelatin dissolves, about 3 minutes. Set aside in the hot water.
3. In a double boiler, beat egg yolks and sugar over barely simmering water until the mixture is light-colored, creamy, and warm to the touch, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and continue to beat until the mixture cools, thickens, and forms a ribbon when the beater is lifted, about 3 minutes. Stir in orange zest and liqueur. Scrape into a large bowl.
4. Beat cream until it is doubled in volume and forms soft peaks.
5. Beat egg whites until they form soft peaks.
6. Stir gelatin mixture into beaten egg yolks. Place the bowl in a larger bowl half-filled with ice and water and stir with a rubber spatula until the mixture begins to thicken and mound, 2 to 4 minutes, depending on the type of bowl used (metal chills faster than glass or ceramic).
7. Immediately remove from ice water and fold in whipped cream.
8. About one-third at a time, fold in egg whites until no streaks of white remain; do not overmix.
9. Spoon half cup mousse mixture into each souffle dish and place a truffle in the center. Divide remaining mousse among dishes to bury truffles. Smooth tops, cover loosely with waxed paper, and place on tray in refrigerator until set and chilled, about 2 1/2 hours.
10. Before serving, remove paper collars. Decorate tops with whipped cream and strips of orange zest, if desired. Serve chilled.
